mumukṣutva.definition: The intense longing or desire for spiritual liberation (mukti) from the cycle of ignorance and birth.

vairāgya.definition: Dispassion, detachment, or desirelessness toward transient worldly and celestial objects.

jñāna.definition: Transcendental knowledge or direct realization of the ultimate nondual reality (Self/Atman/Brahman).

mukti.definition: Liberation, freedom, or release from the illusion of bondage and limited identity.
